jokes.text: So the wacky one?
jokes.setup: Jack asks which picture Liz prefers for his corporate keynote
jokes.punchline: Jack's casual dismissal of his own choice as 'wacky' reveals his corporate mindset
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack characterization - his corporate speak revealing personality quirks

jokes.text: The audience suggestion is... Sling Blade and Oprah on a date.
jokes.setup: Liz mentions performing at corporate retreats with improv
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and bizarre improv prompt
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd specificity that's both random and somehow plausible for bad corporate improv

jokes.text: I sure do like them French-fried 'pataters.'
jokes.setup: Following the Sling Blade improv prompt
jokes.punchline: Jenna's terrible Sling Blade impression
jokes.explanation: Character-driven humor showing Jenna's lack of improv skills

jokes.text: No, you don't, Oprah.
jokes.setup: Jenna's Sling Blade impression
jokes.punchline: Jenna attempting to play Oprah in this bizarre scenario
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity with perfect economy

jokes.text: Jenna is not a great improviser.
jokes.setup: Jenna's terrible improv performance
jokes.punchline: Liz's massive understatement
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery and structure - classic understatement humor

jokes.text: You've got camp jitters.
jokes.setup: Jack's anxiety about the corporate retreat
jokes.punchline: Liz reducing Jack's corporate concerns to summer camp anxiety
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy that punctures corporate pretension with childhood parallel

jokes.text: your little business camp friends
jokes.setup: Liz's camp analogy
jokes.punchline: Doubling down on diminishing Jack's corporate world
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the camp metaphor

jokes.text: don't make me be your camp friend
jokes.setup: Jack asking Liz to accompany him
jokes.punchline: Continuing the camp metaphor with reluctant friendship
jokes.explanation: Solid callback maintaining the metaphor

jokes.text: First of all, never bad-mouth synergy.
jokes.setup: Liz complaining about corporate speak
jokes.punchline: Jack's deadly serious defense of meaningless buzzword
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - treating corporate nonsense with religious reverence

jokes.text: Where you are allowed to eat in the sauna because at some places, they get mad.
jokes.setup: Liz asking about spa amenities
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific requirement revealing Liz's gross habits
jokes.explanation: Perfectly gross Liz moment with believable specificity

jokes.text: Is it a movie where you swap brains with your granddaughter?
jokes.setup: Jenna announcing her movie role
jokes.punchline: Frank's random, specific movie premise
jokes.explanation: Absurd but plausible bad movie premise

jokes.text: Janet Jopler or Janie Jimplin
jokes.setup: Legal issues with using Janis Joplin's name
jokes.punchline: Hilariously bad alternate names
jokes.explanation: Great wordplay with increasingly ridiculous alternatives

jokes.text: If you're nasty.
jokes.setup: Jenna saying to call her 'Miss Joplin'
jokes.punchline: Janet Jackson reference mixed into Janis Joplin method acting
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture mix-up showing Jenna's confusion

jokes.text: Oh, your hands are weird. I don't think you went to high school. I'm not busy Friday. I just said I was.
jokes.setup: Jenna offering last chance to speak to her as Jenna
jokes.punchline: Stream of random, mean observations from the writers
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list of petty grievances

jokes.text: You should do your Janis research on Wikipedia. It's online, so anyone can update it.
jokes.setup: Frank offering helpful research advice
jokes.punchline: Setting up Jenna for false information
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the Wikipedia prank payoff

jokes.text: Did you know that Janis Joplin speed-walked everywhere and was afraid of toilets?
jokes.setup: Frank's Wikipedia suggestion
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake facts about Janis Joplin
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity makes the fake facts funnier

jokes.text: I have heard that, yes.
jokes.setup: Jenna's fake Janis Joplin facts
jokes.punchline: Frank playing along with his own prank
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan commitment to the bit

jokes.text: Dee-ay-butt-ees?
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman trying to deliver serious medical news
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'diabetes' phonetically
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Spaceman moment - incompetent doctor character comedy

jokes.text: Could I replace it with a wheel like Rosie from 'The Jetsons'?
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman warning about losing a foot to diabetes
jokes.punchline: Tracy's cartoon robot solution
jokes.explanation: Absurd but character-appropriate reference

jokes.text: But then, you'd have to register as a motor vehicle.
jokes.setup: Tracy's wheel replacement idea
jokes.punchline: Dr. Spaceman's matter-of-fact response about registration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation treating absurd premise with bureaucratic logic

jokes.text: Because it's winning time, you magnificent son of a bitch!
jokes.setup: Jack psyching himself up in the mirror
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top motivational speak to himself
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Jack's insecurity

jokes.text: Just do it. Is it in you? I'm loving it!
jokes.setup: Jack's self-pep talk
jokes.punchline: String of corporate slogans as motivation
jokes.explanation: Smart escalation using familiar slogans inappropriately

jokes.text: Stop sweating, you idiot. What is wrong with you, you stupid bitch?
jokes.setup: Liz revealing she also does mirror pep talks
jokes.punchline: Her version being self-deprecating and harsh
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast to Jack's self-aggrandizement, very Liz

jokes.text: They're dudes?
jokes.setup: Jack pointing out the Six Sigmas
jokes.punchline: Liz's surprise that business philosophy is embodied by men
jokes.explanation: Good observation about corporate culture

jokes.text: teamwork, insight, brutality... male enhancement... 'handshake-ful-ness' and play hard.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining the Six Sigma business philosophy
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous 'pillars' including made-up words
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of corporate buzzwords with escalating absurdity

jokes.text: Men? If I'd known your wives were alone I would have stayed in New York.
jokes.setup: Jack greeting the Six Sigma men
jokes.punchline: Smooth but inappropriate opening line
jokes.explanation: Good Jack sleazy charm moment

jokes.text: Well, obviously, the Finular analysis will concur with any synergized mindshare classifications. Bladorian frankles.
jokes.setup: Corporate speak about business analysis
jokes.punchline: Complete nonsense words mixed with buzzwords
jokes.explanation: Great corporate speak satire ending in pure gibberish

jokes.setup: Liz insisting she's not any corporate level letter
jokes.punchline: Prashant suggesting an even lower letter
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of corporate hierarchy absurdity

jokes.text: It's a practice wheel for when I lose my foot to diabetes.
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking about something on Tracy's foot
jokes.punchline: Tracy's matter-of-fact explanation of preparation for amputation
jokes.explanation: Good callback with characteristic Tracy logic

jokes.text: There's no link between diabetes and diet. That's a white myth, like Larry Bird or Colorado.
jokes.setup: Kenneth saying Tracy can't eat candy with diabetes
jokes.punchline: Tracy's conspiracy theory including impossible examples
jokes.explanation: Legendary Tracy line - perfectly absurd examples that become quotable memes

jokes.text: My dad had diabetes and he ate whatever he wanted until he died on the day I was born.
jokes.setup: Discussion about diabetes and diet
jokes.punchline: Lutz's darkly personal anecdote missing the obvious connection
jokes.explanation: Dark but character-appropriate, missing obvious causation

jokes.text: most African Americans don't understand that diabetes is caused by sleeping on your back.
jokes.setup: Conspiracy theories about diabetes
jokes.punchline: Toofer adding his own ridiculous medical misinformation
jokes.explanation: Absurd medical claim fitting the escalating misinformation

jokes.text: Next, you'll be telling me that leasing a sports car is a bad investment.
jokes.setup: Kenneth insisting diabetes is diet-related
jokes.punchline: Tracy's sarcastic example that's actually true
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - using obviously bad decision as comparison

jokes.text: I'm going to need some cherry juice, buttermilk and tequila to make my signature Janis Joplin cocktail The Frankschlong.
jokes.setup: Jenna as Janis requesting drink ingredients
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous cocktail name from fake Wikipedia research
jokes.explanation: Good callback with absurd drink name

jokes.text: It's 'LUNCH.' Lego Utilization for Negating Crisis Hierarchies.
jokes.setup: Liz asking what this activity is
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous acronym for simple team building
jokes.explanation: Brilliant corporate acronym satire

jokes.text: No, Lemon, 'CLASS.' It's an acronym for Consuming Lunch And Simple Socializing.
jokes.setup: The LUNCH acronym
jokes.punchline: Another unnecessary acronym for basic activities
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of acronym absurdity

jokes.text: Now, that just seems intentionally confusing.
jokes.setup: Multiple confusing corporate acronyms
jokes.punchline: Liz's obvious observation
jokes.explanation: Good straight-woman reaction to corporate nonsense

jokes.text: N-O-E... no! E.
jokes.setup: Kenneth trying to get Tracy to eat healthy
jokes.punchline: Tracy spelling out his refusal creatively
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay fitting Tracy's character

jokes.text: Oh, you want to hear a tale of the Hill Witch?
jokes.setup: Kenneth's southern superstitions emerging
jokes.punchline: Ominous storytelling voice for made-up threat
jokes.explanation: Great Kenneth character moment setting up elaborate lie

jokes.text: My Mee-maw saw her once taking away a boy who wouldn't eat his vegetables.
jokes.setup: Kenneth starting Hill Witch story
jokes.punchline: Folksy story designed to scare Tracy into eating vegetables
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth character moment with Southern folklore

jokes.text: One look turns you to stone and then she gorges on your living brain.
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing the Hill Witch
jokes.punchline: Logically impossible threat
jokes.explanation: Good absurd threat with logical inconsistency

jokes.text: Through the stone?
jokes.setup: Kenneth's logically impossible threat
jokes.punchline: Tracy catching the logical flaw
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy pointing out the logical flaw

jokes.text: And my page desk will be turned sideways.
jokes.setup: Kenneth listing omens of the Hill Witch
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic mundane 'omen' Kenneth can control
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anticlimax revealing Kenneth's manipulation

jokes.text: I'm gonna do it. I'm going to eat this cat.
jokes.setup: Jenna psyching herself up as Janis
jokes.punchline: Preparing to eat a cat based on fake ALF information
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the prank consequences

jokes.text: That's from 'ALF.' 'ALF' ate cats.
jokes.setup: Jenna about to eat a cat
jokes.punchline: Frank revealing his prank mixing up characters
jokes.explanation: Good revelation of the prank's extent

jokes.text: The Academy loves dead singers and the handicapped and Janis was both!
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining why she wants to play Janis
jokes.punchline: Cynical Oscar strategy with false premise
jokes.explanation: Dark but character-appropriate Oscar cynicism

jokes.text: No, she wasn't. Her legs bent the right way.
jokes.setup: Jenna claiming Janis was handicapped
jokes.punchline: Frank's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan correction

jokes.text: What? I speed-walked home on these!
jokes.setup: Frank correcting Jenna about disabilities
jokes.punchline: Jenna referring to her earlier fake Wikipedia fact
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to the fake Wikipedia facts

jokes.text: I've got a thing for damaged goods.
jokes.setup: Frank being turned on by angry Jenna
jokes.punchline: Frank's honest admission of his fetish
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate creepy revelation

jokes.text: Uh-doy-ee!
jokes.setup: Liz getting into the competitive team building
jokes.punchline: Liz's made-up exclamation of obviousness
jokes.explanation: Quotable Liz neologism that becomes important later

jokes.text: Suck it, nerds!
jokes.setup: Liz getting competitive
jokes.punchline: Liz's surprisingly aggressive celebration
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's competitive side

jokes.text: the familiarity, nickname-ification
jokes.setup: Criticizing Jack's relationship with Liz
jokes.punchline: Made-up corporate word for using nicknames
jokes.explanation: Good corporate speak parody

jokes.text: Sorry, I dropped it when I was pretending it was my penis. Robot penis.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining why she dropped a Lego piece
jokes.punchline: Oversharing inappropriate behavior then clarifying
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with characteristic Liz oversharing

jokes.text: I'm the one who had to take the 'Silkwood' shower this morning.
jokes.setup: Jenna being dramatic about sleeping with Frank
jokes.punchline: Obscure movie reference about contamination
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna dramatic reference

jokes.text: Last night was stupid. Stupid, Frank!
jokes.setup: Jenna trying to downplay hookup with Frank
jokes.punchline: Her insult escalating to include his name awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Shows Jenna's awkward attempt to be dismissive

jokes.text: You... the one who cool should be!
jokes.setup: Frank trying to respond to Jenna's insults
jokes.punchline: Frank's grammatically confused comeback
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate verbal confusion

jokes.text: Aw, you have a sitting area? You jag!
jokes.setup: Liz seeing Jack's hotel room
jokes.punchline: Liz's petty jealousy over hotel amenities
jokes.explanation: Good Liz petty jealousy moment

jokes.text: You can't say 'uh-doy-ee' to me in mixed company.
jokes.setup: Jack criticizing Liz's behavior
jokes.punchline: Specific callback to her earlier exclamation
jokes.explanation: Good callback with corporate propriety concerns

jokes.text: I can go back to the lake with the fat kids and make bracelets.
jokes.setup: Liz feeling rejected by Jack
jokes.punchline: Continuing the summer camp metaphor with self-deprecation
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback extending the camp metaphor with Liz's insecurity

jokes.text: 'Oh, sorry, Liz. My parents' basement only has room for five sleeping bags'
jokes.setup: Liz's hurt feelings
jokes.punchline: Specific childhood rejection scenario
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Liz's childhood issues

jokes.text: I give you a simple managerial suggestion in a professional context and I get back the second half of a Judy Blume novel.
jokes.setup: Jack frustrated with Liz's emotional response
jokes.punchline: Specific literary reference to teen drama
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack line - perfect literary reference for emotional overreaction

jokes.text: I have so much energy my hand keeps dancing!
jokes.setup: Tracy claiming to feel fine
jokes.punchline: Describing obvious diabetic symptoms as positive energy
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Tracy's medical ignorance

jokes.text: This is a victory for hot women everywhere.
jokes.setup: Jenna announcing her hookup with Frank
jokes.punchline: Framing personal embarrassment as feminist triumph
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna self-delusion and grandiosity

jokes.text: What happens in Croton-on-Hudson stays in Croton-on-Hudson.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if Prashant and Joan hooked up
jokes.punchline: Vegas saying applied to mundane corporate retreat location
jokes.explanation: Standard format joke but executed competently

jokes.text: We should start calling ourselves 'The Three Musketeers.' Not.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to bond with her new friends
jokes.punchline: Immediate rejection of her friendship attempt
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Liz's enthusiasm

jokes.text: Now, Dog the Bounty Hunter is the second-grossest guy I've been with.
jokes.setup: Jenna talking about Frank being gross
jokes.punchline: Revealing her history with reality TV star
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna sexual history revelation

jokes.text: Because he's mine.
jokes.setup: Lutz telling Jenna she shouldn't have been with Frank
jokes.punchline: Revealing Lutz's romantic claim on Frank
jokes.explanation: Excellent surprise revelation with perfect economy

jokes.text: Look at me! I'm a monster! The Hill Witch! Cover your brain!
jokes.setup: Kenneth's Hill Witch story setup
jokes.punchline: Kenneth in costume completing his elaborate ruse
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Kenneth's elaborate setup

jokes.text: When a big one falls, four little ones move up.
jokes.setup: Opportunity to help Jack with his microphone
jokes.punchline: Corporate vultures seeing opportunity in Jack's humiliation
jokes.explanation: Dark corporate truth delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Just don't say your name, Jack. Please don't say your name. You are Jack - titan, maverick, lover.
jokes.setup: Jack hoping not to identify himself
jokes.punchline: Immediately saying his name with grandiose descriptors
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic structure with Jack's ego betraying him

jokes.text: I just fooled y'all with my Jack Donaghy impression where I say crazy things that he would never say.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to save Jack from humiliation
jokes.punchline: Claiming it was an impression of things Jack would never say
jokes.explanation: Clever save attempt with logical twist

jokes.text: I believe I heard 'Sling Blade.' Mmm, I love them French-fried 'pataters.'
jokes.setup: Liz trying to distract with impressions
jokes.punchline: Callback to the opening improv scene
jokes.explanation: Good callback bringing the episode full circle

jokes.text: they completely forgot about me when the bra lady went crazy
jokes.setup: Jack explaining how Liz's distraction worked
jokes.punchline: Referring to Liz's performance as 'bra lady going crazy'
jokes.explanation: Good characterization of how others saw Liz's rescue

jokes.text: Uh-doy-ee.
jokes.setup: Liz and Jack reconciling as friends
jokes.punchline: Liz using the phrase Jack criticized, now with affection
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode-ending callback showing relationship repair
